steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence has two independent clauses: "SN 2005ap was a supernova (the explosion of a massive star)" and "the transient yet powerful blast propelled particles and debris into space at extremely high speeds." The blank must close the parenthesis and properly separate these two clauses.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'star) and occurring': "and occurring" turns the second clause into a dangling participial phrase with no main verb — the sentence has no predicate. ✗
  • Choice B — 'star). Occurring': the period ends the first sentence cleanly; "Occurring in the constellation..." then introduces the second sentence as a participial phrase modifying "the transient yet powerful blast." ✓
  • Choice C — 'star), occurring': a comma alone cannot join two independent clauses — this is a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice D — 'star) occurring': no punctuation between the two clauses creates a run-on sentence. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
